heroHeading: Modular Home Extensions, Made for Harrow
metaTitle: Modular Home Extensions in Harrow | EazyBase
metaDescription: Factory-built modular home extensions in Harrow. Designed for you, built in Blackburn in weeks and installed at your home in days, not months.
intro:
Harrow is a borough of homes people hold on to. From the tree-lined Metroland semis around Rayners Lane and North Harrow to family houses in Pinner and Stanmore, this is a place where moving out often feels like a step backwards — the schools, the neighbours, the garden you've spent years getting right. The sensible question isn't "where next?" but "how do we make this house work harder?"

That's exactly what EazyBase modular home extensions in Harrow are designed to do. We design your new kitchen, dining room, home office, playroom or garden room with you, then build it in our Blackburn factory in as little as four weeks — while your home and garden stay untouched. Installation on site typically takes less than a week.

No months of scaffolding, skips and uncertainty. Just a precisely built, fully finished new room, delivered and installed with the kind of care a home you love deserves.

localAngles.heading: Extending a Metroland semi
localAngles.body: So much of Harrow was built in the interwar Metroland years, and those 1930s semis were generous where it matters: decent gardens, solid proportions, room to grow at the rear. A single-storey modular extension sits naturally behind this kind of house — a wider kitchen-diner opening onto the garden, or a proper home office away from the hallway. Because your extension is built off site to precise measurements, it arrives ready to complement the house rather than fight it, with finishes chosen to suit the character of your street.

localAngles.heading: Planning in Harrow, from the Hill to the suburbs
localAngles.body: Harrow spans two very different worlds: the conservation areas around Harrow-on-the-Hill, where extra care and consent are often needed, and the wider suburban streets where permitted development rights often cover single-storey rear extensions — always check with Harrow Council before you commit. We help you understand which side of that line your home sits on early in the process, and we design within realistic limits from day one, so there are no unwelcome surprises once your extension is already in production.

localAngles.heading: Room to grow in Pinner and Stanmore
localAngles.body: Families in Pinner, Stanmore, Hatch End and Kenton often face the same dilemma: the house is one room too small, but the catchment area, the commute and the community are exactly right. Moving means stamp duty, upheaval and — often — leaving a school place behind. Extending means keeping everything that works and adding the space that's missing. A playroom that clears the toys out of the living room, a dining space that fits the whole family, or a quiet office for the working-from-home years. Same address, better house.

localAngles.heading: Why factory-built works in Harrow
localAngles.body: Traditional builds on busy suburban streets are hard on everyone — months of deliveries, skips on the drive and trades coming and going. A factory-built extension turns that on its head. The messy, weather-dependent work happens in our Blackburn facility, built to consistent quality in a controlled environment. What arrives in Harrow is a near-finished room, installed on site in under a week. Your neighbours barely notice, your garden survives, and you're using your new space while a traditional build would still be at foundation stage.

faqs.q: How long does an EazyBase extension take for a home in Harrow?
faqs.a: Your extension is built in our Blackburn factory in as little as four weeks, then transported to Harrow and installed on site in under a week. Compared with a traditional build, you spend far less time living alongside the work — most of the project happens before we ever arrive at your door.

faqs.q: Will I need planning permission in Harrow?
faqs.a: It depends on your home. Permitted development rights often cover single-storey rear extensions, but homes in conservation areas — such as parts of Harrow-on-the-Hill and Pinner — usually need more care, and flats and maisonettes have different rules. We'll help you assess your situation early, and you should always confirm with Harrow Council before work begins.

faqs.q: Can you deliver and install on a typical Harrow street?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes. We carry out a site survey before anything is built, checking access, parking and how the modules will reach your garden. Harrow's suburban streets and driveways are generally well suited to our approach, and because installation takes days rather than months, any disruption to neighbours and parking is short-lived.

faqs.q: What do Harrow homeowners typically use a modular extension for?
faqs.a: The most popular choices locally are open-plan kitchen-diners that connect the house to the garden, dedicated home offices for hybrid working, playrooms for growing families and garden rooms for extra living space. Many of our Harrow customers are families extending rather than moving, so the space is designed around how they actually live.
